Complete trash! How's star bus hateable at best. Her love interest was good, but she tried to destroy him for living her, while she supposedly lived him too. Her best friend, somehow, loved her too...even though she was a wreck of a human and couldn't be trusted for anything other than he Dr skills; he played the kart well. I actually wanted to see more of him just being a Dr and figuring out issues and problems...his dedication to the hateable Dr was just horribly played, horribly written, and obviously down to the modern women's power political ideology, and it was just hard to watch. The wheel chair Dr was good. But it was a character that was forced unnecessary; the writers of ER did the handicap/handcapable/cripple/physically challenged character sooooo much better. The two experienced doctors were done well, but the show tried to play multilingual and it just wasn't done well at all. The two females one learning and the other new, were good in moments but horrific in others. This was the writing the acting was good...tge new Dr was over playing too much, but when the writing calls for it, you have to play it the best you can. The lover boy Dr was good for the part. His competing love interests were better. The story arc there was good and played well. The male nurse that was in charged of the ER was great played like he knew how things worked with out being the magical character that often plagues shows like this. Very well down, wanted much more of his character to be seen. I'm sure I'm missing some characters, if I am, it's probably because you did your job well and didn't stand out in this horrible hospital show.

All in all, there were moments the show was about people that seemed real but the writers did everything they could to try to prevent that. As the top post says, save yourself so time and just watch Pitt on HBO. It was leaps and bounds better than this trash heap.
